title: Event
x-source-of-truth: docs/features/driver-state/spec.md §5 (event schema + canonical encoding) and §6 (state machine / reducer output shapes). This is the leaf contract: types + schema + canonical encoding, no reducer, no ledger mechanism (that is P2). Ship's TS emitter (P5) reproduces the same on-disk shape and canonical hash independently, so the chain rule stays dead simple and the reference vector under testdata/ is the cross-language conformance anchor.
description: One driver-state lifecycle event. Append-only, one file per run, hash-chained (prev links the chain, hash seals the line). Body is a kind-specific payload, schema-shaped by the $defs below and hashed as its raw bytes verbatim. Readers are tolerant: an unknown kind is skipped with a warning, never a hard error (the driver list grok-4.5 lesson).

id.description: evt_<hex> — client-minted idempotency key. NewEventID mints evt_ + 32 hex digits; Append requires only the evt_ prefix so short test-fixture ids keep working.

run.description: dsr_<ulid> — the driver-state run this event belongs to.

v.description: The contract version this event was written against.

kind.description: The lifecycle transition this event records. Enumerated here as the known-v0 set for validation; readers stay tolerant of kinds beyond it (additive within a major version).

stream.description: dss_<ulid> — the stream this event scopes to; empty for run-scoped kinds (run_imported, run_finished).

actor.description: session:<id> | ship:<drv_id> | human:<who> — who wrote the event.

ext_ref.description: Optional top-level external correlate (ship drv_id on run_imported, PR URL on stream_pr_opened) — top-level, not body, so cross-store queries do not parse every body (spec §10 Q2).

body.description: Kind-specific payload; shapes are defined in $defs and validated in Go per kind. Hashed as its raw bytes verbatim — the writer's bytes are the canonical bytes, never re-marshalled.

prev.description: The prior event's hash; empty for the first event in a run.

hash.description: SHA-256 (hex) over the canonical encoding of this event with hash set to the empty string.

run_imported_body.description: The manifest snapshot at import. manifest carries the driver.md frontmatter VERBATIM (render round-trips, and a run with only run_imported resumes from it — spec §7 F3); repo/source/streams are the typed essentials readers index without parsing the snapshot. ship_run_ref carries the optional ship drv_id correlate — field included per spec §10 Q2, semantics deferred.

stream_spec.description: One stream in the imported manifest snapshot.

stream_dispatched_body.description: Locator payload written when a stream is dispatched into a worktree. Every field is optional — older ledgers omit them; a resumer folds the locators into StreamRecord when present.

branch.description: The git branch the dispatch opened, when known.

worktree.description: The absolute path of the dispatch worktree, when known.

engine.description: The dispatching engine (e.g. session) — live ledgers already carry it.

stream_attempt_body.description: One dispatch attempt on a stream. seq is append-only monotone (P2 enforces increase); a terminal attempt with failure_category set lands the stream in failed, absent it lands the stream in landed — no separate stream_failed event required. commit is the head commit the attempt produced, when known.

commit.description: The head commit the attempt produced, when known — used by resume to match ledger state to a PR head SHA.

review_cycle_body.description: One review cycle on an open PR — a first-class kind from v0.1.0 because cycles are retroactively unreconstructable from coarse attempt snapshots.
